hook: Some landlords are clearing 5% net — here is exactly where

slides.title: Beat 5% Net Yield
slides.subtitle: HDB & condo districts where landlord ROI still clears the bar in 2026.

slides.headline: Median condo gross rental yield, island-wide
slides.footnote: URA Rental Index Q4 2025; annualised gross yield on non-landed private residential, island-wide median.

slides.headline: Median HDB gross rental yield, island-wide
slides.footnote: HDB resale & rental data Q4 2025; gross yield = annual rent ÷ median resale price, all flat types.

slides.title: Top 4 Districts for Condo Landlords

items.label: D19 · Serangoon/Hougang
items.sub: Mass-market entry price keeps yields elevated; strong tenant demand from nearby AMK hub.

items.label: D22 · Jurong West
items.sub: Jurong Lake District pipeline lifting rents ahead of capital values; rare yield-growth combo.

items.label: D15 · East Coast
items.sub: Expat tenant base sustains rents; yields compressed by high resale prices but stable.

items.label: D9 · Orchard/River Valley
items.sub: Trophy addresses inflate purchase prices faster than rents; yield drag for pure-income investors.

slides.footnote: SRX rental caveats + URA resale caveats, 12-month trailing avg to Q4 2025. Gross, pre-tax, pre-maintenance.

slides.tag: HDB Gross Yield by Flat Type
slides.title: HDB: Which Flat Type Wins on Yield

items.label: 3-Room
items.sub: Lowest entry price among leasable types; rent-to-price ratio leads the HDB segment clearly.

items.label: 4-Room
items.sub: Sweet spot of tenant demand and price — the most liquid flat type for landlord exit too.

items.label: 5-Room
items.sub: Larger absolute rent but higher resale price offsets; still beats condo island-wide median.

items.label: Executive / Jumbo
items.sub: Premium resale prices compress yield; tenant pool thinner; longer void periods erode net return.

slides.footnote: HDB rental approved transactions + resale caveats Q4 2025. Gross yield. MOP compliance assumed.

slides.title: Why Gross ≠ Net: The Landlord Deductions
items: Property tax (non-owner-occupied rate): 12–36% of annual value from 2024, the single largest drag on net yield.
Maintenance & sinking fund: $200–$600/month for condo reduces gross yield by 0.5–1.0 percentage points.
Void periods: even one month vacant per year cuts 8.3% of gross annual rent — price your lease renewals early.
Agent commission: one month per year tenancy is market norm; factor into your net-yield model before purchasing.
ABSD carrying cost: if you paid 20–60% ABSD to acquire, the hurdle rate for a positive real return rises sharply.

slides.title: Where the 5% Net Threshold Is Actually Cleared
slides.body: At current prices, only HDB 3-room and 4-room flats in OCR and RCR consistently clear 5% net after tax and maintenance. In the condo segment, D19 and D22 come closest — but you must buy sub-$1,350 psf and lock a two-year lease on day one to defend that number. D9 luxury buyers are not yield investors; they are capital-gain bets.
slides.quote: Buy yield where tenants are plentiful and prices are honest — not where addresses are prestigious.

caption:
Some landlords are clearing 5% net yield in 2026 — most are not. Here is the gap. 📈

• HDB 3-room gross yield hits 5.3%; condo island-wide median sits at just 3.6%.
• D19 (Serangoon) and D22 (Jurong West) are the only condo districts near the 5% net bar.
• Property tax, void periods and ABSD drag can erase 1–2% off gross before you notice.

Know your net number before you buy — gross yield headlines are almost always misleading.
